Figure 1:Overview of the proposed latent world model formulation (Sec.3.1).(a) An image tokenizer is first trained with a reconstruction objective to map an input image into compact latent tokens𝒛{\bm{z}}. (Fig.2and Sec.3.2).
(b) Using the learned tokenizer, latent world modelfϕ​(𝒛t,𝒂t)f_{\phi}({\bm{z}}_{t},{\bm{a}}_{t})is trained to model the conditional distribution of the future statepϕ​(𝒛t+1|𝒛t,𝒂t)p_{\phi}({\bm{z}}_{t+1}|{\bm{z}}_{t},{\bm{a}}_{t}), where we adopt masked generative modeling (Sec.3.3).
(c) At test time, the learned latent world model is used fordecision-time planning: An optimization procedure (e.g., MPC with CEM) searches over actions𝒂0:H−1{\bm{a}}_{0:H-1}to minimize the distance between the predicted final state and a goal image.

Figure 2:A tokenizer architecture detail.During training, only the latent resampler and𝒟compact\mathcal{D}_{\textrm{compact}}are updated.ℰψ\mathcal{E}_{\psi}produces masked target tokens (training only), while𝒟ψ\mathcal{D}_{\psi}is used only during inference for pixel level reconstruction.
Table 2:Ablation on CompACT tokenizer.rFID is measured on ImageNet[deng2009imagenet]validation split using clean-fid[parmar2022aliased].

Figure 3:CompACT encoderℰcompact\mathcal{E}_{\textrm{compact}}architecture variation.(a) ViT (scratch)+ [REG]: Initial latent tokens are concatenated to the input patch tokens. This design follows previous transformer-based image tokenizers[yu2024image,bachmann2025flextok,yu2021vector]. (b) DINOv3[simeoni2025dinov3]+ [REG]: Similar to (a), but encoder is initialized with Dinov3. (c) DINOv3[simeoni2025dinov3]+ latent resampler: latent resampler and Dinov3 initialized encoder. Dino and ViT are updated during training in these variants.
Figure 4:Attention visualization for compact latent token in latent resampler.Brighter the color, higher the attention score.

Figure 5:Qualitative results of planning with the proposed CompACT.Best and Worst 1&2 denote the final rollouts corresponding to the simulated trajectories with the minimum and maximum cost, respectively.
Figure 6:Qualitative results of action-conditioned video generation.Red and green boxes indicate incorrect and correct end-effector positions, respectively.
Figure 7:Planning latency breakdown.Comparison of rollout and decoding latency between SD-VAE and CompACT on a single RTX 6000 ADA GPU. Note the log scale on thexx-axis.

Figure 8:𝒟compact\mathcal{D}_{\text{compact}}architecture.The proposed𝒟compact\mathcal{D}_{\text{compact}}is based on MM-DiT[esser2024scaling], a DiT[peebles2023scalable]variant designed for multimodal input processing.
The architecture consists of two parallel processing streams: one for compact latent tokens𝒛{\bm{z}}and another for target latent tokens𝒛ψ{\bm{z}}^{\psi}, which are fused through self attention over the concatenated token sequence. For the overall depiction of CompACT, we refer to Fig. 2 in the main paper.

Figure 9:Inverse Dynamics Model (IDM) architecture.Consecutive frames are tokenized and processed through a transformer-based frame encoder, which produces a single conditioning vector via average pooling. This vector conditions an action denoiser implemented as a diffusion policy[chi2023diffusionpolicy], which predicts the action taken between the two frames.

Figure 10:World modelfϕf_{\phi}architecture for navigation.For navigation tasks, the world model is formulated as an action-conditioned generative model that autoregressively generates the next frame.
The model follows the CDiT architecture proposed in NWM[bar2025navigation], where actions are conditioned via adaptive layer normalization and history frames are conditioned via cross-attention.

Figure 11:World modelfϕf_{\phi}architecture for manipulation.For manipulation tasks, the world model is formulated as a block-causal transformer that enables parallel prediction of multiple future frames conditioned on historical timesteps.
Action tokens are used to condition the prediction head (AdaLN + linear layer) that unmasks latent tokens at each future timestep.
Figure 12:Qualitative results of reconstruction with CompACT.
Figure 13:Attention visualization for compact latent tokens in latent resampler.Brighter the color, higher the attention score.
Figure 14:Plot for ATE, planning latency, and memory peak usage on RECON[shah2021rapid].Latency and memory usage is mesaured for single trajectory optimization, using a single RTX 6000 ADA GPU.
Figure 15:Additional qualitative results of navigation planning with the proposed CompACT.Among candidates, worst two action sequences and best action sequences are presented together.
Figure 16:Additional qualitative results of action-conditioned video generation.Red and green boxes indicate incorrect and correct end-effector positions, respectively.
